description: Opt-in real-corpus search quality queries for comparing ctx search modes on a developer machine with an existing local ctx index.

queries.why_it_matters: Should find release/signing work and prefer dense sessions over one-off Buildkite mentions.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should point to signed metadata or release metadata work, not generic Buildkite setup.

queries.why_it_matters: Known prior investigation with specific provider/schema context.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should include sessions explaining the OpenCode schema mismatch rather than generic schema validation work.

queries.why_it_matters: Tests whether dense performance work is discoverable across sessions.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should discuss incremental import, no-op refresh, catalog caching, or performance thresholds.

queries.why_it_matters: Tests a product/support question with broad terms that can cause noisy expansion.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Useful results should discuss the Codex desktop mobile/remote connection issue, not generic Codex packaging.

queries.why_it_matters: Checks retrieval of deprecated command/history decisions.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should mention deprecated ctx context/context-packet behavior or removal rationale.

queries.why_it_matters: Has specific option names and can reveal whether exact search is enough.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should mention search refresh behavior or include-current-session filtering.

queries.why_it_matters: Control for query expansion over several generic implementation terms.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Good results should discuss provider metadata, citations, source path availability, or source_exists fields together.

queries.why_it_matters: Tests whether security/redaction history is found without overmatching generic terms.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should discuss redaction oracles, secrets, or path redaction.

queries.why_it_matters: Checks discoverability of MCP tool output decisions.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should mention MCP research/search tools or structuredContent output.

queries.why_it_matters: Tests higher-level design process retrieval.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should point to CLI design policy/principles or private agent-facing docs decisions.

queries.why_it_matters: Known remaining issue where active-session exclusion can miss grandchildren.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Useful results should discuss parent/root session metadata or nested subagent exclusion.

queries.why_it_matters: Checks retrieval for status latency optimization.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should mention status speed, source counting, or SQLite status performance.

queries.why_it_matters: Tests historical discussion of storage maintenance.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Useful results should discuss VACUUM, optimize, WAL, DB size, or import compaction.

queries.why_it_matters: Tests broad product/security topic retrieval.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should discuss cloud search security, end-to-end encryption, or homomorphic encryption feasibility.

queries.why_it_matters: Known provider-source edge case.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should explain Gemini source discovery without importable transcript rows.

queries.why_it_matters: Checks install UX and release verification retrieval.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should discuss install script, ctx.rs/install, or release verification.

queries.why_it_matters: Checks skill/plugin installation context.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should mention ctx-agent-history-search skill/plugin installation or Codex plugin cache.

queries.why_it_matters: Tests repo history/process query with uncommon wording.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should discuss public history reset, orphan backup, or force-push plan.

queries.why_it_matters: Checks transcript-mode default decision retrieval.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should mention lite transcript default and full/log transcript modes.

queries.why_it_matters: Checks MCP server product boundary.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should mention read-only MCP tools, no refresh/import, or structured JSON.

queries.why_it_matters: Known bad experience that should be retrievable.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should discuss search hanging, CPU usage, timeout, or performance tests.

queries.why_it_matters: Checks exact numeric/storage query and DB-size discussion.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should distinguish original session corpus size from ctx SQLite DB size.

queries.why_it_matters: Known missing performance-test part of the product.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should mention no-op incremental catch-up/import and timing.

queries.why_it_matters: Tests natural-language fuzzy memory rather than known keywords.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Good results should either find a real deprecated module discussion or clearly avoid misleading confident matches.

queries.query: what did we delete after the outage
queries.why_it_matters: Tests broad causal question where exact search may fail and expansion can be noisy.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Good results should surface outage/removal sessions only if those concepts occur together.

queries.query: why did it try to reimport everything
queries.why_it_matters: Known user question with conversational wording.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should discuss import all, incremental import, catalog state, or current-session refresh behavior.

queries.why_it_matters: Specific security/release question that should reward exact phrase and related terms.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Top results should discuss signed CLI metadata, public keys, or metadata signing.

queries.why_it_matters: Control query for removed/internal product language that should not dominate current public UX.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Useful results may be historical, but current search should not treat this as a recommended public surface.

queries.why_it_matters: Control for false positives under keyword expansion.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Best behavior is zero results or clearly irrelevant low-confidence results.

queries.why_it_matters: Measures latency/noise on a very broad term.
queries.expected_quality_signal: Results may be numerous; ranking should still avoid useless repetition and remain fast.
